metadata.description: Expanded integration tests including newly added datasets (2025-2026)

test_categories.gene_mapping: Gene symbol/ID to various resources (proteins, transcripts, GO terms)
test_categories.drug_discovery: Pathway-based and tissue-specific drug target discovery
test_categories.pathways: Pathway and network analysis (Reactome, STRING, Rhea)
test_categories.ontologies: Ontology navigation (GO, EFO, taxonomy, UBERON, CL hierarchies)
test_categories.protein_interactions: Molecular interaction networks (IntAct): protein-protein, protein-small molecule, protein-RNA, protein-gene, mRNA-miRNA
test_categories.gene_expression: Tissue and cell-specific expression (Bgee, UBERON, CL)
test_categories.ncbi_datasets: NCBI/Entrez Gene, RefSeq, PubChem compound mappings
test_categories.data_validation: Data quality validation - attribute correctness, counts, known facts
test_categories.antibody: Therapeutic and structural antibody data (TheraSAbDab, SAbDab, IMGT)
test_categories.clinical_trials: Clinical trial data and disease ontology mappings (ClinicalTrials.gov, MONDO)
test_categories.pubchem_drug: PubChem drug discovery paths (bioactivity, targets, metabolites, CTD)
test_categories.variant_pharmacogenomics: Variant analysis and pharmacogenomics (dbSNP, PharmGKB, AlphaMissense)
test_categories.disease_drug: Disease to drug discovery workflows (MONDO, ChEMBL, DrugCentral, antibodies)
test_categories.expression_celltype: Expression analysis (Bgee, CellXGene, SCXA) and cell type ontology
test_categories.pathway_network: Pathway and network analysis (Reactome hierarchy, STRING, GO reverse)
test_categories.ontology_hierarchy: Ontology hierarchy traversal (MONDO, HPO, EFO parent/child)
test_categories.known_issues: Tests for known bugs - expected to fail until fixed

tests.name: Gene Symbol to Uniprot
tests.why: Core mapping: researchers start with gene symbols and need protein IDs for downstream analysis

tests.name: Gene Symbol to Reviewed Uniprot (Swiss-Prot)
tests.why: Get only manually curated Swiss-Prot entries for higher confidence protein data

tests.name: Gene Symbol to Human Reviewed UniProt
tests.why: Human-specific mapping to Swiss-Prot - used in AlphaFold and IntAct paths for precise mapping

tests.name: Gene to GO Biological Process
tests.why: Understand what biological processes a gene is involved in - essential for functional annotation

tests.name: Gene to GO Molecular Function
tests.why: Identify molecular activities (kinase, binding, etc.) - critical for drug target analysis

tests.name: Ensembl Gene to Transcripts
tests.why: Find all transcript isoforms - critical for alternative splicing analysis and RNA-seq

tests.name: Gene to Protein Coding Transcripts
tests.why: Filter only protein-coding isoforms, excluding ncRNAs for proteomics workflows

tests.name: Cancer Genes to Uniprot via HGNC
tests.why: Cancer gene panel analysis - get curated protein entries for tumor suppressor genes

tests.name: Protein to InterPro Domains
tests.why: Map proteins to functional domains - essential for understanding protein architecture and mutations

tests.name: Protein to InterPro Families
tests.why: Identify protein families for evolutionary analysis and functional prediction

tests.name: Gene to Mutation Features
tests.why: Find experimentally verified mutations - critical for variant interpretation and cancer research

tests.name: Gene to Sequence Variants
tests.why: Get known protein variants for clinical variant interpretation and population genetics

tests.name: Protein to PDB Structures
tests.why: Find 3D structures for drug design, protein engineering, and structural biology

tests.name: Protein to NMR Structures
tests.why: Get solution structures (NMR) vs crystal structures - different conformational states

tests.name: Protein to Reactome Pathways
tests.why: Identify biological pathways - critical for systems biology and drug target prioritization

tests.name: Protein to Signaling Pathways
tests.why: Focus on signaling cascades - key for cancer biology and targeted therapy

tests.name: Transcript to Exons
tests.why: Get exon structure for splice variant analysis and exome sequencing

tests.name: Gene to CDS (Coding Sequences)
tests.why: Get coding sequences for codon usage analysis and ORF prediction

tests.name: Gene to Orthologs
tests.why: Find orthologs across species for comparative genomics and model organism studies

tests.name: Human Gene to Mouse Orthologs
tests.why: Map human genes to mouse for translational research and model validation

tests.name: Gene to Paralogs
tests.why: Find gene duplications within species - important for functional redundancy studies

tests.name: Affymetrix Probe to Transcript
tests.why: Map microarray probes to current transcripts - essential for legacy data reanalysis

tests.name: Affymetrix Probe to Gene Symbol
tests.why: Convert probe IDs to gene symbols for publication and meta-analysis - HGNC info is embedded in ensembl attributes

tests.name: Compound to ChEMBL Activities
tests.why: Get bioactivity data for compound - essential for SAR analysis and lead optimization

tests.name: Compound to IC50 Activities
tests.why: Filter for IC50 measurements - standard potency metric for drug discovery

tests.name: Compound to High-Potency Activities
tests.why: Find highly active compounds for hit prioritization

tests.name: ChEMBL Document to Assays
tests.why: Get experimental assays from publications - useful for protocol reproduction

tests.name: ChEMBL Assay to Target Proteins
tests.why: Map assays to protein targets for mechanism of action studies

tests.name: ChEMBL Target to UniProt
tests.why: Link drug targets to protein sequences for structure-based drug design

tests.name: Cancer Genes to ChEMBL Targets
tests.why: Find drugs targeting cancer genes - critical for precision oncology

tests.name: GO Term to Parent Terms
tests.why: Navigate ontology hierarchy upward - useful for enrichment analysis rollup

tests.name: GO Term to Biological Process Parents
tests.why: Filter parent terms by aspect - maintain biological process semantics

tests.name: Taxonomy to Children Taxa
tests.why: Find sub-species or strains - important for microbiome and comparative studies

tests.name: Taxonomy Grand-children (2 levels down)
tests.why: Navigate deeper in taxonomy tree for detailed organism classification

tests.name: Taxonomy to Grand-parent
tests.why: Navigate up taxonomy tree to find broader classifications

tests.name: EFO Disease to Child Terms
tests.why: Find disease subtypes - useful for disease classification and phenotype mapping

tests.name: EFO Disease to Parent Terms
tests.why: Navigate to broader disease categories for meta-analysis grouping

tests.name: GO Term to Genes (via Ensembl)
tests.why: Find all human genes with specific GO annotation - reverse lookup for function

tests.name: Protein to All Pathways
tests.why: Get complete pathway context for protein - systems biology analysis

tests.name: Protein to Activation Pathways
tests.why: Focus on activation mechanisms - key for understanding signal transduction

tests.name: Protein to Regulation Pathways
tests.why: Find regulatory pathways - important for feedback loops and control mechanisms

tests.name: Gene to STRING Interactions
tests.why: Build protein interaction networks - essential for network biology and target discovery

tests.name: Molecule Search by SMILES
tests.why: Convert chemical structure to database ID - critical for cheminformatics

tests.name: Molecule Search by InChI Key
tests.why: Standard chemical identifier lookup - for database integration

tests.name: ChEMBL Molecule to PubChem (UniChem)
tests.why: ChEMBL molecule to PubChem compound via UniChem mappings - enables disease→compound queries

tests.name: EFO Disease to ChEMBL to PubChem
tests.why: Disease → ChEMBL molecule → PubChem compound path for drug discovery

tests.name: ZINC ID Search (Virtual Screening)
tests.why: Search by ZINC database ID for virtual screening compound lookup

tests.name: Gene to ENA mRNA Sequences
tests.why: Get mRNA sequences for cloning and expression studies

tests.name: Gene to RefSeq IDs
tests.why: Map to NCBI RefSeq - standard reference sequences for genomics

tests.name: Entrez Gene to Ensembl
tests.why: Convert NCBI Gene IDs to Ensembl - cross-database integration

tests.name: Entrez Gene to GO Terms
tests.why: Functional annotation from Entrez Gene ID - legacy data integration

tests.name: Protein to IntAct Interactions
tests.why: Find experimentally validated protein-protein interactions for network analysis

tests.name: UniProt to IntAct Interactions
tests.why: Direct UniProt to IntAct mapping - used in IntAct path for protein interaction queries

tests.name: Gene to Protein Interactions via UniProt
tests.why: Map genes to interaction partners - essential for pathway and network biology

tests.name: Protein to Interaction Partners Details
tests.why: Get detailed protein information for interaction partners

tests.name: IntAct to GO Biological Process
tests.why: Functional annotation of interaction partners - pathway context for networks

tests.name: ChEBI to IntAct Interactions (Protein-Small Molecule)
tests.why: Find protein interactions with small molecules - essential for drug-target analysis

tests.name: ChEBI to Interacting Proteins via IntAct
tests.why: Map small molecules to their protein targets - drug mechanism of action

tests.name: RNAcentral to IntAct Interactions (Protein-RNA)
tests.why: Find protein-RNA interactions - critical for RNA biology and RBP analysis

tests.name: RNAcentral to Interacting Proteins via IntAct
tests.why: Map RNAs to their binding proteins - essential for RNA-protein complex analysis

tests.name: IntAct Interaction Lookup by ID
tests.why: Direct lookup of interaction details by IntAct ID

tests.name: Protein to Small Molecule Partners via IntAct
tests.why: Find small molecules that interact with a protein - ligand discovery

tests.name: Ensembl Gene to IntAct Interactions (Protein-Gene)
tests.why: Find protein-gene interactions where a gene product interacts with proteins - captures regulatory interactions

tests.name: Transcript to IntAct Interactions (mRNA-miRNA)
tests.why: Find mRNA-miRNA interactions where Ensembl transcripts interact with miRNAs (RNAcentral)

tests.name: Transcript to miRNA Partners via IntAct
tests.why: Map mRNA transcripts to their regulatory miRNAs via IntAct interactions

tests.name: Gene to Bgee Expression Data
tests.why: Get tissue-specific expression patterns across anatomical structures

tests.name: Tissue to Expressed Genes via Bgee
tests.why: Find genes expressed in specific tissue - tissue marker discovery

tests.name: Cell Type to Expressed Genes
tests.why: Identify genes expressed in specific cell types - single-cell markers

tests.name: Gene to Tissues Where Expressed
tests.why: Map gene to anatomical locations - expression atlas

tests.name: Gene to Cell Types Where Expressed
tests.why: Identify cell-type specific expression - scRNA-seq validation

tests.name: UBERON Parent Tissues
tests.why: Navigate anatomy hierarchy upward - from specific to general structures

tests.name: UBERON Child Structures
tests.why: Navigate anatomy hierarchy downward - find sub-structures

tests.name: Cell Ontology Parents
tests.why: Navigate cell type hierarchy - from specific to general cell types

tests.name: Cell Ontology Children
tests.why: Find cell subtypes - detailed cell type classification

tests.name: Reactome Pathway Lookup
tests.why: Get curated pathway information with participants and hierarchy

tests.name: Protein to Reactome Pathways
tests.why: Find all pathways involving protein - systems biology analysis

tests.name: Reactome Pathway Parents
tests.why: Navigate pathway hierarchy upward - find broader pathway categories

tests.name: Reactome Pathway Children
tests.why: Navigate pathway hierarchy downward - find sub-pathways

tests.name: Reactome to Proteins
tests.why: Get all proteins involved in pathway - network reconstruction

tests.name: Rhea Reaction Lookup
tests.why: Get biochemical reaction details with substrates and products

tests.name: ChEBI Compound to Rhea Reactions
tests.why: Find reactions involving specific chemical compound

tests.name: Disease Trait to GWAS SNPs via EFO
tests.why: Find genetic variants associated with disease - reverse lookup from phenotype

tests.name: Study to Associated SNPs
tests.why: Get all SNP associations from GWAS study publication

tests.name: SwissLipids Lipid Lookup
tests.why: Get lipid structure and biological context

tests.name: AlphaFold Structure Lookup
tests.why: Get predicted 3D structure for protein

tests.name: Gene to AlphaFold via UniProt
tests.why: Map genes to AlphaFold predicted structures for drug discovery - structure-based drug design workflow

tests.name: UniProt to AlphaFold Structure
tests.why: Direct UniProt to AlphaFold mapping for structural analysis

tests.name: AlphaFold Attributes Validation
tests.why: TP53 AlphaFold structure should have pLDDT confidence metrics

tests.name: Complete Network: Gene to Interactions to Pathways
tests.why: Build complete functional network - interaction partners and their pathways

tests.name: Gene to BioGRID Interactions via Entrez
tests.why: Map genes to BioGRID protein-protein interactions - critical for network analysis and drug discovery

tests.name: Entrez Gene to BioGRID Interactions
tests.why: Direct Entrez Gene ID to BioGRID mapping for interaction network analysis

tests.name: BioGRID Interaction Lookup by ID
tests.why: Direct lookup of BioGRID interaction entry by BioGRID ID

tests.name: BioGRID to UniProt Interactors
tests.why: Get UniProt IDs for BioGRID interaction partners - enables protein feature analysis of interactors

tests.name: UniProt to BioGRID Interactions
tests.why: Direct UniProt to BioGRID mapping for drug target interaction enrichment

tests.name: Multi-hop: Tissue to Genes to Functions
tests.why: Tissue-specific functional enrichment - find functions of genes in tissues

tests.name: Gene to ClinVar Variants via Ensembl
tests.why: Map genes to clinical variants - critical for clinical genetics and variant interpretation. NOTE: HGNC is embedded in Ensembl, so use ensembl path not hgnc.

tests.name: Ensembl Gene ID to ClinVar Variants
tests.why: Direct Ensembl gene ID to ClinVar mapping for clinical research

tests.name: Cancer Gene Panel to ClinVar
tests.why: Batch query cancer genes to ClinVar for clinical panel analysis (Lynch syndrome genes)

tests.name: Gene to ClinVar to UniProt Features
tests.why: Multi-hop: gene to clinical variants with protein feature context

tests.name: ClinVar Variant to MONDO Disease
tests.why: Map ClinVar variants to MONDO disease ontology for disease classification

tests.name: MONDO Disease to ClinVar Variants
tests.why: Find all ClinVar variants associated with a specific MONDO disease term

tests.name: ClinVar Variant to MeSH Terms
tests.why: Map ClinVar variants to MeSH disease descriptors for medical literature search

tests.name: ClinVar Variant to HPO Phenotypes
tests.why: Map ClinVar variants to Human Phenotype Ontology terms for phenotype analysis

tests.name: ClinVar Variant to OMIM
tests.why: Map ClinVar variants to OMIM/MIM entries for Mendelian disease research

tests.name: GO Kinase Activity to Proteins
tests.why: Find proteins with kinase activity - prime drug targets for cancer therapy

tests.name: GO DNA Repair to Proteins
tests.why: DNA repair pathway proteins - targets for PARP inhibitors and cancer therapy

tests.name: Reactome Metabolism to Proteins
tests.why: Metabolism pathway proteins for metabolic disease drug targets

tests.name: Reactome Signal Transduction to Drug Targets
tests.why: Signal transduction pathway to druggable protein components

tests.name: Brain-Specific Expression to Proteins
tests.why: Brain-expressed genes to proteins for neurological drug discovery

tests.name: GO Apoptosis to Protein Domains
tests.why: Apoptosis pathway genes to domain annotations for structural analysis

tests.name: Reactome Immune System to Interactions
tests.why: Immune pathway proteins to interaction networks for immunotherapy targets

tests.name: EFO Heart Disease to GWAS
tests.why: Heart disease trait to GWAS SNP associations

tests.name: EFO Diabetes to GWAS
tests.why: Diabetes trait to GWAS SNP associations for genetic risk analysis

tests.name: Cancer Genes to Orthologs Batch
tests.why: Batch query cancer genes to orthologs for model organism studies

tests.name: Gene to dbSNP Variants
tests.why: Map genes to dbSNP variants for genetic variation analysis - core SNP discovery workflow

tests.name: Ensembl Gene ID to dbSNP
tests.why: Direct Ensembl gene ID to dbSNP variant mapping

tests.name: dbSNP Variant Lookup by rsID
tests.why: Direct lookup of dbSNP variants by rs ID - essential for SNP annotation

tests.name: dbSNP to ClinVar via Gene
tests.why: Find clinical significance of dbSNP variants via gene to ClinVar mapping

tests.name: Gene Symbol to Entrez to UniProt
tests.why: Map gene symbols via NCBI Entrez Gene to UniProt proteins - alternative to Ensembl route

tests.name: Gene Symbol to Entrez to GO
tests.why: Gene function via NCBI gene2go annotations - comprehensive GO mapping

tests.name: Gene Symbol to Entrez to RefSeq
tests.why: Map to NCBI RefSeq reference sequences for gene and transcript data

tests.name: Gene Symbol to Entrez Orthologs
tests.why: Find orthologous genes across species via NCBI gene orthologs

tests.name: Gene Symbol to Entrez Related Genes
tests.why: Find functionally related genes via NCBI gene groups

tests.name: Gene Symbol to Entrez Neighboring Genes
tests.why: Find genomically neighboring genes via NCBI gene neighbors

tests.name: PubChem Compound Lookup
tests.why: Search PubChem compounds by CID - rich chemical attributes (SMILES, InChI, FDA status)

tests.name: RefSeq mRNA Lookup (versioned)
tests.why: Search RefSeq mRNA with version - full attributes (type, status, symbol, description)

tests.name: RefSeq mRNA Lookup (unversioned)
tests.why: Search RefSeq mRNA without version - works but no attributes

tests.name: RefSeq Protein Lookup
tests.why: Search RefSeq protein accessions directly

tests.name: Entrez Gene to Ensembl to ClinVar
tests.why: Multi-hop from Entrez gene to clinical variants via Ensembl

tests.name: RefSeq to Entrez
tests.why: RefSeq accessions should map back to Entrez Gene IDs - needs outgoing links from RefSeq

tests.name: RefSeq to UniProt
tests.why: RefSeq to UniProt mapping - needs gene_refseq_uniprotkb_collab.gz integration

tests.name: PubChem to BioActivity
tests.why: PubChem compound to bioactivity data (assay results)

tests.name: PubChem to Gene Target via Activity
tests.why: PubChem compound to gene targets via bioactivity data

tests.name: PubChem to Protein Target via Activity
tests.why: PubChem compound to protein targets via bioactivity data. NOTE: Can be slow for compounds with many activities (e.g., aspirin has 3689 cross-refs)

tests.name: PubChem to HMDB
tests.why: PubChem compound to HMDB metabolite cross-reference

tests.name: PubChem to Patent
tests.why: PubChem compound to patent cross-reference

tests.name: PubChem to ChEMBL Molecule
tests.why: PubChem compound to ChEMBL molecule cross-reference (extracted from synonyms like CHEMBL25)

tests.name: PubChem to BindingDB
tests.why: PubChem compound to BindingDB cross-reference (extracted from BDBM synonyms)

tests.name: PubChem to Patent Compound (SureChEMBL)
tests.why: PubChem compound to SureChEMBL patent compound cross-reference (extracted from SCHEMBL synonyms)

tests.name: PubChem to CAS
tests.why: PubChem compound to CAS Registry Number cross-reference

tests.name: Entrez Gene ID Direct to Ensembl
tests.why: Map numeric Entrez Gene IDs directly to Ensembl - WORKS! 672=BRCA1, 7157=TP53

tests.name: Aspirin Attributes
tests.why: Validate PubChem compound attributes for aspirin (CID 2244)

tests.name: Aspirin External Database Identifiers
tests.why: Validate PubChem external database identifiers extracted from synonyms for aspirin (CID 2244). NOTE: These fields may be empty if synonyms don't contain these identifiers.

tests.name: Entrez Human BRCA1 Attributes
tests.why: Validate Entrez gene attributes for human BRCA1 (Gene ID 672)

tests.name: RefSeq BRCA1 Attributes
tests.why: Validate RefSeq attributes for BRCA1 mRNA

tests.name: BRCA1 ClinVar Count
tests.why: BRCA1 should have many ClinVar variants (>100)

tests.name: TP53 GO Count
tests.why: TP53 should have many GO annotations (>50)

tests.name: Aspirin Cross-ref Count
tests.why: Aspirin should have many cross-references (>1000) including bioactivities

tests.name: TP53 to P04637 Known Fact
tests.why: TP53 gene should encode UniProt protein P04637

tests.name: EGFR to P00533 Known Fact
tests.why: EGFR gene should encode UniProt protein P00533

tests.name: BRCA1 to P38398 Known Fact
tests.why: BRCA1 gene should encode UniProt protein P38398

tests.name: Aspirin to HMDB Known Fact
tests.why: Aspirin should link to HMDB metabolite database

tests.name: Type 2 Diabetes MONDO to GWAS Genes
tests.why: Find genes associated with Type 2 Diabetes via GWAS studies - disease genetics workflow

tests.name: Disease to GWAS Genes via EFO (Human)
tests.why: Full disease to human genes path via GWAS - used in dbSNP path for gene retrieval

tests.name: Glioblastoma Text to GWAS Genes
tests.why: Drug discovery workflow: disease text to genes via GWAS - tests text-based disease lookup. MONDO:0005044 is main glioblastoma (not subtypes like MONDO:0000457)

tests.name: Disease to ClinVar Genes via MONDO (Human)
tests.why: Full disease to human genes path via ClinVar - used in dbSNP path for gene retrieval

tests.name: Glioblastoma Text to ClinVar Genes
tests.why: Drug discovery workflow: disease text to genes via ClinVar - tests text-based disease lookup. MONDO:0005044 is main glioblastoma (not subtypes like MONDO:0000457)

tests.name: Protein to Pathogenic ClinVar Variants
tests.why: Filter ClinVar variants to only pathogenic classifications - critical for clinical variant interpretation and identifying disease-causing mutations

tests.name: Gene to Pathogenic ClinVar Variants
tests.why: Find pathogenic variants for cancer genes - essential for clinical genetics panels and hereditary cancer testing

tests.name: Gene to Likely Pathogenic ClinVar Variants
tests.why: Find likely pathogenic variants - important for variant classification in clinical reports

tests.name: Gene to SNV ClinVar Variants
tests.why: Filter ClinVar to only single nucleotide variants - useful for SNP-focused analysis

tests.name: Gene to Deletion ClinVar Variants
tests.why: Filter ClinVar to deletion variants - important for detecting loss-of-function mutations

tests.name: ClinVar Pathogenic Variant Attributes Validation
tests.why: RB1 pathogenic variants should have correct classification attribute - filter ensures only pathogenic variants returned

tests.name: JAK2 Advanced Clinical Trial Drugs
tests.why: Find JAK2 drugs in advanced clinical trials (Phase 3/4) - full drug discovery chain. NOTE: This is a long chain query and may be slow (~60s)

tests.name: Disease Text to AlphaFold Structures
tests.why: Drug discovery workflow: disease text to AlphaFold structures for structure-based drug design

tests.name: Disease Text to BioGRID Interactions
tests.why: Drug discovery workflow: disease text to protein interactions for network-based target discovery

tests.name: Disease Text to dbSNP Variants
tests.why: Drug discovery workflow: disease text to genetic variants for pharmacogenomics

tests.name: Disease Text to IntAct Interactions
tests.why: Drug discovery workflow: disease text to experimentally validated protein interactions

tests.name: Therapeutic Antibody Lookup
tests.why: Therapeutic antibodies (TheraSAbDab) should be directly queryable by INN name

tests.name: Therapeutic Antibody to Target Genes
tests.why: Therapeutic antibodies should link to their target genes via composite target notation splitting (e.g., ITGA2B/CD41 -> ITGA2B)

tests.name: Structural Antibody via PDB
tests.why: PDB structures should link to SAbDab antibody entries for structural antibody data

tests.name: Gene Target to Therapeutic Antibodies
tests.why: Find all therapeutic antibodies targeting a specific gene - critical for competitive landscape analysis and drug discovery

tests.name: Antibody Attributes Validation
tests.why: Bevacizumab should have correct attributes including target VEGFA and therapeutic source

tests.name: Antibody to EFO Disease Ontology
tests.why: Therapeutic antibodies with indications should map to EFO disease ontology via text lookup

tests.name: Antibody to MONDO Disease Ontology
tests.why: Therapeutic antibodies with indications should map to MONDO disease ontology via text lookup

tests.name: EFO Disease to Therapeutic Antibodies
tests.why: Find therapeutic antibodies approved for specific diseases - drug repurposing and competitive analysis

tests.name: MONDO Disease to Therapeutic Antibodies
tests.why: Find therapeutic antibodies for MONDO diseases - enables disease-centric drug discovery

tests.name: Clinical Trial Lookup
tests.why: Clinical trials should be directly queryable by NCT ID

tests.name: Clinical Trial to MONDO Disease
tests.why: Clinical trials with disease conditions should map to MONDO ontology via text lookup

tests.name: MONDO Disease to Clinical Trials
tests.why: MONDO disease terms should link to clinical trials studying that disease - enables disease-centric trial discovery

tests.name: Disease Text Search to Clinical Trials
tests.why: Disease names should find clinical trials via text search - primary discovery workflow

tests.name: Disease to Clinical Trials via MONDO
tests.why: Find clinical trials for disease terms via MONDO ontology mapping - handles partial names

tests.name: Clinical Trial to Gene Targets via MONDO
tests.why: Multi-hop: find gene targets for diseases studied in clinical trials

tests.name: Clinical Trial Attributes Validation
tests.why: Clinical trial should have correct attributes including phase and study type

tests.name: Clinical Trial MONDO Count Validation
tests.why: NCT06777108 (Hypertension trial) should have at least 1 MONDO mapping

tests.name: Gene to PubChem Compounds via Bioactivity
tests.why: Find PubChem compounds with bioactivity against gene targets - complementary to ChEMBL

tests.name: Gene to FDA-Approved PubChem Compounds
tests.why: Find FDA-approved drugs from PubChem with bioactivity against gene targets

tests.name: PubChem Compound to Gene Targets
tests.why: Find gene targets for a PubChem compound

tests.name: PubChem Compound to Protein Targets
tests.why: Find protein targets for a PubChem compound

tests.name: PubChem to HMDB Metabolites
tests.why: Cross-reference PubChem compounds to HMDB metabolite database

tests.name: PubChem to ChEMBL Cross-Reference
tests.why: Cross-reference PubChem to ChEMBL for combined drug data

tests.name: PubChem to Literature References
tests.why: Find PubMed literature references for PubChem compounds (63k+ for aspirin)

tests.name: PubChem to Patents
tests.why: Find patent information for PubChem compounds

tests.name: PubChem to BindingDB
tests.why: Cross-reference to BindingDB for binding affinity data (Ki, IC50)

tests.name: PubChem to CTD (Toxicogenomics)
tests.why: Map compounds to CTD for toxicogenomics data (gene-chemical-disease relationships)

tests.name: PubChem to PharmGKB
tests.why: Cross-reference to PharmGKB for pharmacogenomics annotations

tests.name: Protein to PubChem Activities
tests.why: Find all PubChem bioactivity data for a protein target

tests.name: PubChem Activity to Gene Target
tests.why: Map PubChem bioactivity to gene targets

tests.name: MONDO Disease to CTD Compounds
tests.why: Disease to compounds via CTD toxicogenomics - enables drug discovery via gene-chemical-disease relationships

tests.name: CTD to MONDO Disease
tests.why: Reverse mapping from CTD chemical-disease to MONDO ontology

tests.name: dbSNP to GWAS Association
tests.why: SNP to GWAS association is a core variant analysis use case

tests.name: Gene to PharmGKB Clinical Annotations
tests.why: Gene to clinical annotations for pharmacogenomic dosing guidelines

tests.name: Drug to CPIC Guidelines
tests.why: CPIC dosing guideline retrieval for pharmacogenomics

tests.name: Protein to AlphaMissense Predictions
tests.why: Get all AI-predicted pathogenic missense variants for a protein

tests.name: GWAS Study to SNPs
tests.why: Get all SNPs from a GWAS study

tests.name: ClinVar to Disease (MONDO)
tests.why: Variant to disease association lookup

tests.name: Disease to Therapeutic Antibodies
tests.why: Find approved or in-development therapeutic antibodies for diseases

tests.name: DrugCentral via UniProt
tests.why: Find all approved drugs targeting a specific protein

tests.name: ChEMBL to DrugCentral
tests.why: Map ChEMBL compounds to DrugCentral approved drugs via InChIKey lookup

tests.name: PubChem to DrugCentral
tests.why: Map PubChem compounds to DrugCentral approved drugs via InChIKey lookup

tests.name: Huntington Disease Gene Associations
tests.why: Huntington disease should have strong GenCC gene associations

tests.name: Full ChEMBL Chain - BRAF
tests.why: BRAF is a validated oncology target with many drugs - test full 7-step chain

tests.name: Gene to Tissues via Bgee
tests.why: Map gene to all tissues where it is expressed

tests.name: Gene to Cell Types via Bgee
tests.why: Map gene to cell types with expression

tests.name: Cell Type to CellXGene Datasets
tests.why: Find single-cell datasets containing specific cell types

tests.name: Tissue to CellXGene Datasets
tests.why: Find single-cell datasets for specific tissues

tests.name: Disease to CellXGene Datasets
tests.why: Find disease-specific scRNA-seq datasets

tests.name: Gene to SCXA Experiments
tests.why: Map gene to Single Cell Expression Atlas experiments

tests.name: Cell Type to SCXA Experiments
tests.why: Find SCXA experiments with specific cell types

tests.name: Pathway Hierarchy Child
tests.why: Test pathway hierarchy navigation downward

tests.name: Pathway Hierarchy Parent
tests.why: Test pathway hierarchy navigation upward

tests.name: Reactome to UniProt Direct
tests.why: Map Reactome pathways directly to participating proteins

tests.name: GO to Proteins (Reverse Lookup)
tests.why: Find proteins with specific molecular function

tests.name: Protein to STRING Network
tests.why: Map UniProt to STRING for network analysis

tests.name: STRING to Protein Partners
tests.why: Map STRING ID back to UniProt interaction partners

tests.name: Protein Interaction Network via IntAct
tests.why: Find protein interaction partners via IntAct

tests.name: Protein Interaction Network via BioGRID
tests.why: Find BRCA1 interaction partners via BioGRID

tests.name: MONDO Parent Traversal
tests.why: Verify upward traversal in MONDO disease hierarchy

tests.name: MONDO Child Enumeration
tests.why: Verify downward traversal to find disease subtypes

tests.name: HPO Parent Traversal
tests.why: Verify upward traversal in HPO phenotype hierarchy

tests.name: HPO Child Enumeration
tests.why: Verify downward traversal to find phenotype subtypes

tests.name: EFO to MONDO Mapping
tests.why: Verify cross-ontology mapping from EFO traits to MONDO diseases

tests.name: MONDO to GenCC Gene Curations
tests.why: Verify disease to gene-disease curation mappings

tests.name: MONDO to GWAS Associations
tests.why: Verify disease to GWAS catalog associations

tests.name: MeSH Parent Traversal
tests.why: Navigate MeSH tree hierarchy upward - find broader descriptor categories

tests.name: MeSH Child Enumeration
tests.why: Navigate MeSH tree hierarchy downward - find narrower descriptors

tests.name: MeSH Entry Lookup
tests.why: Basic MeSH descriptor lookup by ID

tests.name: MeSH Descriptor Attributes
tests.why: Verify MeSH descriptor has expected attributes (name, tree numbers, scope note)

tests.name: MeSH Parent Count Validation
